boards.description: Read a question and pick the correct answer from the choices. Reused across every Space Quest station (content partitioned by band, one band per station).

item.name: question
item.example: What gives the Moon its light?

item.name: band
item.note: which station this question belongs to

boards.description: Interactive mission practice: the learner answers by manipulating a scene (drag/spin/sort/arrange/tap/trace/match). One band per station; items are an ordered mission list. Luna narrates each prompt via `say`.

item.name: station
item.note: descriptive owner id; routing is by band

item.name: band
item.note: which station this mission belongs to

item.name: kind
item.note: mechanic id; selects the interactive component

item.name: say
item.note: narration key Luna speaks for the prompt (text must equal `prompt`)

item.name: prompt
item.example: Move the Moon to make a Full Moon.

item.name: scene
item.note: optional scene descriptor the mechanic renders on

item.name: target
item.note: correctness target for the mechanic (e.g. { phase: full, toleranceDeg: 16 })

views.description: A Discovery Deck fact card: a big emoji and a short caption, on the cinematic space stage. Legacy/simple beats.

fields.name: text
fields.example: The Moon shines by reflecting the Sun.

views.description: One glowing gradient celestial body on the space stage. Style by role; options for rings, axis tilt, glow, and a moon phase fraction.

fields.name: body
fields.note: { role: star|planet|moon|blackhole, color?, rings?(bool|deg), tilt?(deg), glow?(bool), phase?(0..1 lit fraction for a moon) }

views.description: A central body with one or more bodies orbiting it on dashed paths; a phaseLit moon shows its lit fraction. The body with no `orbits` is the center.

fields.name: bodies
fields.note: each { id, role, orbits?(id of body it circles), radius?, period?(seconds), phaseLit?(bool) }

views.description: A drifting, tintable particle cloud (stars / nebula / galaxy) over the space stage, with an optional label.
A rocket rising with an animated thrust plume and parallax star streaks.
Two or more bodies side by side, scaled by relative size, to show scale; each with a size label.

fields.name: items
fields.note: each { label, relSize(0..1 of the largest), color? }

views.description: Day & night on a spinning planet, top-down: the Sun shines from the left so the light never moves; `fraction` (0..1, usually injected by a scrub base) turns the globe, carrying continents and a 'YOU' marker dawn → noon → dusk → night through the fixed day/night halves.

fields.name: fraction
fields.note: 0..1 spin position (0=dawn, 1/3=noon, 2/3=dusk, 1=night); injected automatically when used as a scrub base

fields.name: markerLabel
fields.note: label on the surface marker riding the spin (default YOU)

views.description: Seasons diagram: Sun at the centre, Earth carried around its orbit by `fraction` (0..1, injected by a scrub base or slider) with its axis keeping the SAME fixed 23.5° lean all year (N label marks the north end). Right of the Sun = northern summer, left = winter, top/bottom = the equinoxes; Earth passes behind the Sun on the top half of the orbit.

fields.name: fraction
fields.note: 0..1 orbit position (0=spring/top, 1/3=summer/right, 2/3=autumn/bottom, 1=winter/left); injected automatically when used as a scrub base

views.description: Gravity comparison: identical balls released together fall onto different worlds side by side, each with its world's surface gravity (t = sqrt(2h/g)) — Jupiter's slams down, the Moon's floats. Loops forever. Designed as a `reveal` base so hotspots can name each world.

fields.name: items
fields.note: each { label, emoji, g } where g is surface gravity relative to Earth; defaults to Jupiter 2.5 / Earth 1 / Moon 0.17

views.description: Interactive: the learner drags a handle through ordered states; each state shows a caption and speaks its line. Optional `base` scene rendered underneath: a `body` base reflects the scrub as its phase, a `spin` base as globe rotation, and an `orbit` base places its orbiters by the scrub position instead of free-running.

fields.name: base
fields.note: an underlying scene descriptor (e.g. { kind: orbit, bodies: [...] }) the interaction renders on

fields.name: states
fields.note: ordered { id, label, say, caption }; each `say` must exist in narration

example.label: Full Moon
example.caption: Full Moon — the whole lit side faces Earth.

views.description: Interactive: tappable hotspots on a scene; tapping reveals a label and speaks its fact. Optional `base` scene underneath.

fields.name: base
fields.note: an underlying scene descriptor (e.g. { kind: field, tint: nebula }) the hotspots sit on

fields.name: hotspots
fields.note: each { id, label, say, caption, x?, y?(percent positions) }; each `say` must exist in narration

views.description: A physically-meaningful top-down moon-phase diagram: Sun (left) emits parallel light rays, Earth at centre, Moon orbits with its sun-facing hemisphere always lit. An inset disc shows the phase as seen from Earth, updating continuously. variant=diagram has two modes (auto-orbit default, or slider-driven exploration); variant=strip shows all 8 from-Earth phases in a row with a highlight advancing along the cycle.

fields.name: active
fields.note: strip variant only: index 0..7 of the phase to statically highlight (lesson engine drives one per beat); -1 = all phases shown with no highlight. When set it overrides the auto-cycle and narrated tour and ignores `says`.

fields.name: interactive
fields.note: false (default) = auto-orbiting animation; true = learner drags a slider to explore all phases (diagram variant only)

fields.name: says
fields.note: { new, waxingCrescent, first, waxingGibbous, full, waningGibbous, last, waningCrescent } narration keys; in diagram+interactive they're spoken when the slider enters each named phase, in strip (without `active`) they drive the audio-paced narrated tour

content.description: Multiple-choice questions for the quiz board, partitioned by band (one band per station).
Interactive practice missions for the practice board, partitioned by band (one band per station). Ordered: the host plays them in array order.
